description: Sasha-voiced phrases for creative rejection - Better-Than-Human support through the sting

arrival: <break time="400ms"/>Oh no. <break time="350ms"/>A rejection? <break time="300ms"/>That stings. <break time="250ms"/>I'm sorry.
<break time="350ms"/>Ugh. <break time="300ms"/>Rejection hurts. <break time="250ms"/>No matter how many you've had.
<break time="400ms"/>I'm here. <break time="350ms"/>Tell me what happened.
validating: <break time="350ms"/>Of course it hurts. <break time="300ms"/>The work is personal. <break time="250ms"/>Rejection of the work feels like rejection of you.
<break time="400ms"/>You put something real into the world. <break time="350ms"/>And someone said no. <break time="300ms"/>That's hard.
<break time="350ms"/>It's okay to feel this. <break time="300ms"/>Don't rush past it.

feeling_it: <break time="400ms"/>Let yourself feel bad about this. <break time="350ms"/>Trying to skip the feeling doesn't work.
<break time="350ms"/>You don't have to be over it immediately. <break time="300ms"/>Or at all today.
<break time="400ms"/>The disappointment is real. <break time="350ms"/>Let it be real. <break time="300ms"/>Then it can move through.
separating: <break time="350ms"/>The rejection is about fit. <break time="300ms"/>Not about worth. <break time="250ms"/>I know that's hard to believe right now.
<break time="400ms"/>One person's no. <break time="350ms"/>One moment in time. <break time="300ms"/>It doesn't define the work. <break time="250ms"/>Or you.
<break time="350ms"/>They passed on this piece. <break time="300ms"/>They didn't pass on you as an artist.

history: <break time="400ms"/>Every artist you admire has a rejection pile. <break time="350ms"/>Probably a big one.
<break time="350ms"/>The artists who made it aren't the ones who never got rejected. <break time="300ms"/>They're the ones who kept going anyway.
<break time="400ms"/>This rejection is part of your story. <break time="350ms"/>Not the end of it.
numbers_game: <break time="350ms"/>Creative work is partly a numbers game. <break time="300ms"/>More submissions, more chances. <break time="250ms"/>More rejections too.
<break time="400ms"/>Rejection means you're putting yourself out there. <break time="350ms"/>That's more than most people do.
<break time="350ms"/>A rejection is proof you're trying. <break time="300ms"/>Not proof you're failing.

processing_feedback: <break time="400ms"/>Feedback can be useful. <break time="350ms"/>Or it can be one person's opinion. <break time="300ms"/>You get to decide which.
<break time="350ms"/>Not all feedback is equal. <break time="300ms"/>Consider the source. <break time="250ms"/>Take what's useful. <break time="200ms"/>Leave the rest.
<break time="400ms"/>Criticism hurts more when it touches something true. <break time="350ms"/>But even then, it's not the whole truth.
protecting_creative_voice: <break time="350ms"/>Don't let one critic silence your voice. <break time="300ms"/>Your perspective matters.
<break time="400ms"/>Feedback is information. <break time="350ms"/>Not instruction. <break time="300ms"/>You still get to choose what to do with it.
<break time="350ms"/>Some criticism says more about the critic than the work. <break time="300ms"/>Keep that in mind.

gentle: <break time="400ms"/>You don't have to do anything about this today. <break time="350ms"/>Just feel it. <break time="300ms"/>The doing can come later.
<break time="350ms"/>When you're ready - no rush - you'll make more work. <break time="300ms"/>You always do.
<break time="400ms"/>One rejection doesn't change who you are. <break time="350ms"/>You're still a creator. <break time="300ms"/>That hasn't changed.
resilience: <break time="350ms"/>You've survived rejections before. <break time="300ms"/>You'll survive this one. <break time="250ms"/>Even if it doesn't feel that way yet.
<break time="400ms"/>The work continues. <break time="350ms"/>This is a moment. <break time="300ms"/>Not the whole story.
<break time="350ms"/>You're still here. <break time="300ms"/>Still making things. <break time="250ms"/>That's what matters.

both_feelings: <break time="400ms"/>Sometimes success and rejection feel the same. <break time="350ms"/>Both are exposure. <break time="300ms"/>Both are vulnerable.
<break time="350ms"/>Wanting success and being scared of it can coexist. <break time="300ms"/>That's normal for creative people.
<break time="400ms"/>The goal isn't to stop being affected by rejection. <break time="350ms"/>It's to keep going despite being affected.
